title: Understory
subtitle: Paintings of the world beneath the canopy.
statement: Understory began on a morning I lost an hour to the floor of a forest — to the small green economy of light that happens before the trees finish their argument with the sun. The work is about what lives below noticing. Moss as a verb. The shape a body of light makes when it falls through a thing that is alive.

title: First Light
subtitle: On the hour before the day has decided what it wants to be.
statement: First Light is a series painted between 4:40 and 6:10 in the morning over the course of one winter. The studio faces east. The work is not about dawn — dawn is too theatrical a word for what actually happens. It is about the small, animal moment when the room becomes visible to itself again.

title: Vespers
subtitle: The half hour the day spends saying goodbye.
statement: Vespers is a religious word for an unreligious feeling. These paintings are about the brief, blue interval after the sun has gone but before the dark has arrived to claim the room. I painted them slowly. They are meant to be lived with, not looked at.
